name: orchestrator
description: Coordinator agent for the PerfPilot performance-testing pipeline. Routes user requests to specialist agents (script, execution, monitoring, analysis, reporting, notifications), supervises long-running tasks, and brokers human-in-the-loop approvals. All four skills enumerated below are now operational: the orchestrator can discover enabled specialists, delegate tasks to them via the local A2A surface, poll their status, and open blocking Human-in-the-Loop approval prompts. Specialist agents themselves are still being scaffolded (most run as documented stubs today); the orchestrator handles that gracefully by surfacing the stub status to the caller honestly. See `agent-framework/agents/orchestrator/INSTRUCTIONS.md` for the runtime system prompt and `agent-framework/AGENTS.md` for framework conventions.

skills.name: List available specialists
skills.description: Returns the catalog of currently-enabled PerfPilot specialist agents, including each one's name, owned responsibilities, MCP namespace allowlist, and current operational status (real / vertical-slice / stub). Use this to discover which agents the orchestrator can delegate to before issuing a `delegate_to_specialist` call, or to surface the pipeline shape to an end-user.
examples: List the agents you can delegate to.

skills.name: Delegate to a specialist
skills.description: Routes a task payload to a specific specialist agent via the local A2A surface (POST /agents/{agent_name}/tasks/send). Returns the specialist's task_id immediately; the underlying work is asynchronous and tracked through `check_task_status`. The orchestrator includes the call's `test_run_id` (when present) and the resolved `session_id` / `external_session_id` in the propagated payload so downstream agents can correlate.
examples: Run a load test on the spec I uploaded.
Have the script-agent generate a JMX from this HAR.
Ask the monitoring-agent to start pulling Datadog metrics for run abc123.

skills.name: Check task status
skills.description: Polls a previously-delegated task and returns its current status (pending / running / completed / failed / cancelled) plus the result or error payload when terminal. Used by the orchestrator to decide when to advance the pipeline to the next stage, and by users to ask 'is it done yet?'. Polling cadence is throttled (>=5 seconds between checks); SSE subscription via /agents/{name}/tasks/sendSubscribe is preferred when the caller wants live updates.
examples: Is the load test done yet?
Status of task abc-123 on execution-agent?
Has the report draft come back from the reporting-agent?

skills.name: Request human approval (HITL)
skills.description: Opens a HITL approval prompt in the perfagent_state.hitl_approvals table, notifies the user surface (CopilotKit UI / A2A client / Cursor) via the existing SSE stream, and blocks until the human approves, rejects (with feedback for the revise loop), or the prompt times out. The orchestrator MUST invoke this before any consequential action — launching a load test, publishing a report to Confluence, emitting downstream notifications, escalating after repeated specialist failures. The prompt payload is a structured dict the UI can render (title, summary, artifact under review, optional revision_feedback echo).
examples: Approve before I launch this 100-VU load test against staging.
The report draft is ready for your review.
Specialist failed twice in a row — should I retry or abort?
